method.dns_rule: All other shift types are marked DO_NOT_SCHEDULE.
method.fallback_rule: Members with no matched normalized history receive a peer-pattern fallback built from aggregate historical patterns by employment status, blended with certification, so everyone has some grounded future availability.
method.history_rule: If a member ever worked a shift type in normalized history, mark that shift type AVAILABLE in future.
method.preferred_rule: Top 6 historically most common shift types are marked PREFERRED. If fewer than 6 existed, all historical shift types become PREFERRED.

manual_import_notes: 2026-05 whiteboard import: only May 1-31 imported; listed names set to preferred; all other active members set to do_not_schedule for each AM/PM block.

2026-06 whiteboard import: June A/B/C/D calendar used as canonical 24-hour AEMT preference baseline; A=Sophie, B=Lynnsey, C=open, D=Barbara; assigned AEMT marked preferred for both AM and PM, all others do_not_schedule.

2026-06 whiteboard import: June A/B/C/D calendar used as canonical 24-hour AEMT weighting baseline; A=Sophie, B=Lynnsey, C=open, D=Barbara; shift-model AEMTs marked preferred for assigned track days and available for off-track days; C days remain open with no preferred track AEMT.

2026-06 whiteboard import: June A/B/C/D calendar used as canonical 24-hour AEMT weighting baseline; A=Sophie, B=Lynnsey, C=open, D=Barbara; shift-model AEMTs marked preferred for assigned track days; all other active members left blank as no-selection state; C days remain open with no preferred track AEMT.
